summary: EQT AB agreed to acquire Australia’s Orikan, saying it will help the smart parking technology company invest in innovation, strengthen its business and expand into adjacent markets.

title: Europe’s Heat Wave Is Bright Spot for China’s Portable AC Makers
summary: As heat records shattered across Europe in the last weeks of June and indoor temperatures rose to uncomfortable levels, many of the continent’s residents sought immediate relief in the form of portable air conditioner units.

title: Mamoon Hamid on Learning from Startups
summary: Barry sits down with Kleiner Perkins Partner, Mamoon Hamid. They discuss Mamoon's thoughts on the AI revolution and his approach to early AI investing.  Mamoon also breaks down how he became an early investor in giants like Slack, Figma, and how Kleiner Perkins assesses the investments they missed.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ondas to Buy Autonomous Aircraft Maker DZYNE for $875.8 Million
summary: Ondas Inc. agreed to buy autonomous aircraft maker DZYNE Technologies in an $875.8 million cash-and stock-deal that positions Ondas as a full-service autonomous defense technology platform.

title: Aluminum Gains Despite Goldman Warning of Faster Supply Rebound
summary: Aluminum rose from near a four-month low, even as Goldman Sachs Group Inc. warned that supply from Middle Eastern smelters is rebounding more quickly than expected.

title: Tech Lifts US Stock Futures After Holiday Break: Markets Wrap
summary: US futures signaled a bounce in technology stocks at the start of a week in which South Korea’s memory giants will put the artificial-intelligence trade to yet another test.

title: US Investors Are Finally Getting a Clear Shot at SK Hynix
summary: Until now, it has been difficult, if not impossible, for American investors to bet on SK Hynix.

title: Carry Traders Shift Away From Dollar in Emerging-Market Bets
summary: Emerging-market traders are increasingly turning to currencies from the euro to the Australian dollar to fund bets in the developing world as the US dollar roars back.

title: Sara Duterte Faces Impeachment Trial With Philippine Presidential Dream At Stake
summary: Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte faces a contentious trial in the Senate, a case that could end her political career or leave her as the top contender to succeed archrival Ferdinand Marcos Jr. as president.

title: EasyJet’s Stock Shows Castlelake Bid May Be Far From a Slam Dunk
summary: Castlelake LP’s latest bid to buy EasyJet Plc reached £6.90 per share in cash, but the UK airline is trading below that even with Monday’s post-announcement bump, signaling investors may be concerned about the potential regulatory and political barriers facing the £5 billion-plus deal.

title: The Investors Scrambling to Keep SpaceX - and Elon Musk - Out of Their Portfolios
summary: Christopher Bejnar has spent the last couple of months combing through the fine print of exchange-traded funds, emailing financial advisers and moving money into European stocks – all to keep SpaceX out of his $1 million portfolio.

title: Samsung High-Stakes Results to Set Mood for Chip Stock Bulls
summary: A wild ride for global chip stocks in recent weeks has left investors looking for fresh validation of the artificial intelligence trade. Samsung Electronics Co. may provide just that on Tuesday.

title: Canadians Skeptical of Carney’s Push to Open Airports to Private Investment
summary: A slim majority of Canadians oppose allowing private investment in airports, a new poll shows, as Prime Minister Mark Carney explores selling stakes in the publicly owned facilities to unlock funding for major infrastructure projects.

title: Citi Joins Small Group of Banks Running London’s Gold Vaults
summary: Citigroup Inc. has joined the small group of banks that provide the vaulting and clearing services that underpin the gold market in London, the world’s biggest bullion-trading center.

title: Gold Falls After Weekly Gain as Rate-Hike Concerns Recede
summary: Gold dropped after posting its first weekly advance since May on weakening expectations that the US Federal Reserve will hike interest rates.

title: The Tanker Tycoon Making Millions on Hormuz Shuttle Runs
summary: Just a few weeks into the war, one of the Persian Gulf’s top oil producers quietly began sneaking its crude out of the Strait of Hormuz. Before long, the covert project became so successful that the United Arab Emirates was already approaching its pre-war rate of flows through the waterway by the time the US and Iran signed their interim peace deal.

title: Hungary Selling First Eurobond to Harvest Post-Orban Rally
summary: Hungary is selling its first international bond since Viktor Orban’s ouster in April’s watershed election triggered a plunge in borrowing costs.

title: Options Flash Bearish Yen Signal as Tokyo Stays on the Sidelines
summary: The options market is sending a bearish signal on the yen, with interest-rate differentials remaining firmly in favor of the dollar and the Japanese authorities avoiding intervention for now.

title: Morgan Stanley’s Wilson Sees Rotation From Chips to Hyperscalers
summary: US stocks will struggle to reach new highs as investors rotate out of some of this year’s biggest winning technology trades, according to Morgan Stanley strategists.

title: Stalled AI Rally Has More Eyeing Old-Guard Stocks, Markets Pulse Survey Says
summary: Growing doubts about sustainability of the AI rally are pushing more investors toward old-economy stocks, according to the latest Markets Pulse survey.

title: Are SpaceX and Tesla Hurtling Toward a Merger?
summary: There’s growing speculation that Elon Musk will combine his two biggest companies.

title: Taiwan Authorities Search Evergreen Marine Premises
summary: Taipei prosecutors and the Investigation Bureau searched Evergreen Marine Corp.’s premises on Monday morning as part of a probe into suspected insider trading, according to multiple media reports.

title: Intrum Returns to Europe’s Junk Bond Market After Equity Raise
summary: Swedish debt collector Intrum AB is back in Europe’s high-yield bond market for the first time since a sharp jump in financing costs forced it into a restructuring.

title: Ocado CEO Steiner to Step Down in 2028 After Board Tussle
summary: Ocado Group Plc Chief Executive Office Tim Steiner will step down at the start of its 2028 financial year, following a boardroom tussle at the British online grocery delivery company mired in a stock slump.

title: Oil Fluctuates as Hormuz Flows Continue
summary: Oil swung in a narrow range as flows through the Strait of Hormuz persisted and OPEC+ signaled higher supplies. Bloomberg's Stephen Stapczynski reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: South African Businesses Bet on Renewables
summary: Businesses in South Africa are investing in renewable energy to protect against rolling blackouts and steep price increases from state-owned power utility Eskom Holdings. Bloomberg's Jennifer Zabasajja reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Goldman Cuts Yen Forecast to 165 Per Dollar, Likes Carry Trades
summary: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. sees the yen weakening to 165 per dollar in a year’s time, driven in part by Japan’s interest rate differentials with the US.

title: Samsung Earnings Pivotal for Korea Bulls: 3-Minutes MLIV
summary: Anna Edwards, Guy Johnson, Tom Mackenzie and Paul Dobson break down today's key themes for analysts and investors on "Bloomberg: The Opening Trade."  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: These Are the Sharps Actually Making Money on Prediction Markets
summary: What it takes to win on Kalshi and Polymarket.

title: Odd Lots: The Sharps Who Dominate Prediction Markets (Podcast)
summary: Here’s a couple things about prediction markets. A lot of it is pure gambling and speculation, much of it on things with very little economic relevance. Another fact is that in all likelihood, if you yourself started trading right now, you’d probably lose your shirt. But there is money being made by some dedicated traders, really focused on areas like politics and economics. On this episode, we speak with Brian Golden and Daniel Reichman, who are part of a private Discord called MAGA Kiwi Club,

title: Japan Former FX Czar: Yen Undervalued by Up to 20%
summary: The yen should be as much as 20% stronger than it is, said former top foreign exchange official in Japan Tatsuo Yamasaki. He spoke in an exclusive interview with Bloomberg’ Shery Ahn in Tokyo. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Goldman Sachs Sees Yen at 165 per Dollar in 12 Months
summary: Kamakshya Trivedi, head of global FX and interest rates at Goldman Sachs, discusses the firm's forecast for the yen which it has revised to 165 per dollar in a year’s time from 155. "I think the undervaluation that you're seeing on the yen is really historic," Trivedi tells Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)
